description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly gray and white coloration, featuring streaks and patches of brown and tan. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible textural variations and some areas appearing more granular or pitted. The stone has a somewhat elongated, oval form, tapering at one end and bulging at the other, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. Subtle striations and layering are visible, suggesting sedimentary origins or weathering effects. The stone's exterior shows signs of exposure to the elements, with some smoother worn areas and others more jagged or chipped. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a minor obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: An irregular, rough-textured gray and white stone with brown streaks and a natural, weathered appearance. Its elongated, oval form and layered surface make it visually distinct.
description_view_0: The stone appears rounded at the front, with a prominent bulge and visible surface texture, highlighting its natural, uncut form.
description_view_1: Seen from the side, the stone looks narrow and reveals more of its layered, striated pattern with a mix of gray, white, and brown tones.
description_view_2: This view emphasizes the stone's broad, oval side, showing granular texture and a blend of colors with pronounced surface irregularities.
description_view_3: From the opposite side, the stone tapers and displays more weathered, rough areas, with some visible markings and a slightly pointed end.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and layering with mottled patches and streaks, giving a natural, sedimentary look.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with some smoother, worn areas and visible pitting, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, often partially embedded or surrounded by low vegetation or mulch.
